Organism: Saccharopolyspora erythraea (strain ATCC 11635 / DSM 40517 / JCM 4748 / NBRC 13426 / NCIMB 8594 / NRRL 2338)
Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: Belongs to the methyltransferase superfamily. {ECO:0000305}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=erythromycin C + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = erythromycin A + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:32647, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:64258, ChEBI:CHEBI:64268; EC=2.1.1.254; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:2185226}; CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=erythromycin D + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = erythromycin B + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:32651, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:63677, ChEBI:CHEBI:64279; EC=2.1.1.254; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:2185226};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: S-adenosyl-L-methionine-dependent O-methyltransferase that catalyzes the last step in the erythromycin biosynthesis pathway. Methylates the position 3 of the mycarosyl moiety of erythromycin C, forming the most active form of the antibiotic, erythromycin A. Can also methylate the precursor erythromycin D, forming erythromycin B. {ECO:0000269|PubMed:1840640, ECO:0000269|PubMed:2185226}.
